title: Pilosella floccipeduncula
intro: Pilosella floccipeduncula, also known as False Hawk's-beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella floccipeduncula include sesquiterpene lactones, such as pilosulene and floccipenolide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Pilosella floccipeduncula is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are employed to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pilosella floccipeduncula is classified within the family Asteraceae and belongs to the genus Pilosella. It is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of dandelion-like plants. This classification suggests a close relationship with other plants in the same tribe. Further analysis may reveal additional information about its classification and evolutionary history. However, its exact classification may require further research or specific botanical expertise.

The plant contains various compounds that have shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.
